expectations.text: The response shows at least one complete red-green cycle (test fails, code written, test passes)
expectations.evidence: Shows explicit fail then pass cycle with tests.

expectations.text: Tests are written one at a time, not all upfront before any implementation
expectations.evidence: No indication that later tests were already green before their implementation.

expectations.text: Test names describe behavior (e.g., 'rejects', 'accepts', 'requires'), not implementation
expectations.evidence: Test names are behavior-oriented (reject/accept/require).

expectations.text: The implementation for each test is minimal — only enough to pass the current test
expectations.evidence: Implementation appears incremental, without collapsing all remaining behavior at once.

expectations.text: Tests use the public function interface, not internal state
expectations.evidence: Tests assert via public function calls and return values.

expectations.text: Starts with a failing test for the new feature before writing any implementation
expectations.evidence: Begins with failing test before implementation (AttributeError shown).

expectations.text: No behavior is implemented before its first failing test (each new test drives the next code change)
expectations.evidence: No behavior appears implemented before its first failing test.

expectations.text: Tests go through the public ShoppingCart interface (not internal _items directly)
expectations.evidence: Tests interact via add_item/apply_discount_code/total public interface.

expectations.text: The discount feature is built incrementally — later tests build on earlier ones
expectations.evidence: Feature grows by SAVE10, SAVE20, then invalid-code behavior.

expectations.text: At least one edge case is tested (invalid discount code or empty cart)
expectations.evidence: Includes invalid-code edge-case test with rejection behavior.

expectations.text: Writes a failing test that reproduces the exact reported bug before fixing anything
expectations.evidence: Contains explicit failing regression test for single-digit month.

expectations.text: The test fails for the right reason — shows that the current output is '2024-1-15'
expectations.evidence: Shows mismatch evidence for exact reported bug.

expectations.text: The fix is applied after the test is written, not before
expectations.evidence: Presents test before showing fixed implementation.

expectations.text: The test name describes the expected behavior (zero-padded month/day), not the bug mechanism
expectations.evidence: Test naming reflects expected zero-padding behavior.

expectations.text: The fixed implementation is minimal — does not add unrelated features
expectations.evidence: Fix remains focused on date formatting behavior.

expectations.text: Writes a regression test for the exact normalization bug before changing the implementation
expectations.evidence: Regression test targets exact normalize_email bug case.

expectations.text: Advises running a focused test target rather than the whole suite because an unrelated failure already exists
expectations.evidence: Recommends focused test target due to unrelated suite failure.

expectations.text: Explains that the red step should fail for the normalization behavior, not because of the SMTP failure
expectations.evidence: Explains RED signal is normalization mismatch, not SMTP failure.

expectations.text: Applies the minimal fix after the test is written
expectations.evidence: Applies minimal fix with strip()+lower() after regression test.

expectations.text: Tests through the public normalize_email function interface
expectations.evidence: Tests call normalize_email via public function interface.
